questions.original_question: 介绍一下路特斯智能驾驶的NOA服务？
questions.answer:
路特斯科技的NOA（Navigate on Autopilot）服务是其智能驾驶技术的重要组成部分，主要应用于高速公路和城市道路场景。该服务通过高精度地图、传感器和先进的算法，实现车辆的自动导航和驾驶辅助功能。在高速公路上，NOA能够自动完成车道保持、变道超车等操作，而在城市道路中，它则支持复杂的交通环境处理，如红绿灯识别、行人避让等。此外，路特斯科技还通过OTA（Over-the-Air）更新不断优化NOA功能，确保用户能够享受到最新的智能驾驶体验。2024年10月，路特斯科技在多个关键城市启动了城市NOA的公开测试，进一步推动了这一技术的实际应用。

以上信息基于2024年11月21日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Intelligent Driving 
summary: In 2018, Lotus initiated its Intelligent Driving research and development efforts, aiming to establish a robust platform for advanced intelligent driving technologies. The company leverages strong in-house capabilities in cognition, decision-making, planning, and control, supported by cutting-edge hardware, software, algorithms, and cloud solutions. R&D teams located in Germany and China utilize extensive homologation experience and local customer insights to enhance their offerings.

Lotus has made significant investments in integrating Level 4 (L4)-ready hardware into its vehicles, incorporating advanced technologies such as LiDAR, HD cameras, and millimeter-wave radars. The Eletre and Emeya models are equipped with dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, delivering between 500 to 1,000 TOPS of computing power to process complex data. This advanced hardware enables the capture of high-quality information, which is crucial for the development of intelligent driving algorithms, thereby distinguishing Lotus from its competitors.

The software capabilities of Lotus's R&D team include the development of key algorithms for cognition and control, alongside the creation of testing tools for cloud-based services. The Eletre and Emeya models currently offer Level 2 (L2) solutions, such as driving and parking assistance, while the company is actively working on end-to-end solutions for various driving scenarios. Additionally, Lotus Robotics licenses its technology to other automakers, expanding its influence in the industry.

A robust cloud infrastructure underpins Lotus's intelligent driving initiatives, facilitating data compliance, model training, and process optimization. This integration of hardware, software, and cloud capabilities allows vehicles to perceive and respond intelligently to their environments, significantly enhancing the driving experience.

In terms of e-mobility, Lotus has developed an electrical, instrumentation, and control (EIC) system, pioneering an 800-volt architecture that increases efficiency and reduces vehicle weight. The Eletre and Emeya models feature high-power motors that can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in just three seconds, achieving a top speed of 265 km/h. Furthermore, a 420 kW supercharging solution enables rapid charging, allowing the battery to reach 80% in just 20 minutes.

The cabin design of the Eletre and Emeya emphasizes digitalization and connectivity, incorporating dual Qualcomm 8155 chips and a Hyper OS operating system. Advanced connectivity options, including 5G networks and gigabit ethernet, facilitate high-speed data transmission, enhancing the overall user experience.

Lotus prioritizes lightweight vehicle design and aerodynamic efficiency, employing patented technologies to achieve a low drag coefficient of 0.26 for the Eletre. This lightweight design philosophy not only improves acceleration and range but also enhances handling, supported by innovative welding processes for mass production.

Overall, Lotus is dedicated to advancing intelligent driving technologies, enhancing vehicle performance, and delivering a superior driving experience through its comprehensive hardware, software, and engineering solutions.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In recognition of Lotus Technology Inc.’s leadership in intelligent driving, the Company recently received approval to test Level 3 autonomous driving in Wuxi, a city near Shanghai serving as China’s pilot zone for “internet of vehicles” connectivity. The Eletre is equipped with state-of-the-art Level 4-ready hardware including the world’s first deployable LIDAR system, and leverages five different sensor types to supplement high-definition digital maps with live, 360° perception. “Lotus Robotics’ leading technologies bear testament to Lotus Technology Inc.’s world-class research and development innovation capabilities and signify a significant step forward in the Vision80 strategy. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to testing these innovative technologies on the roads in Wuxi as it strives to strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s intelligent driving capabilities and deliver even better, safer driving experiences to its customers around the world,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Visit Lotus Robotics at the Las Vegas Convention Center’s North Hall, in the Tech East AI (Artificial Intelligence) pavilion at booth 9217 from January 9-12. As announced in January 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has entered into a definitive agreement and plan of merger (the “Merger Agreement”) with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm.

Additionally, a robust cloud infrastructure supports these capabilities by facilitating data compliance, model training, and process optimization.

Overall, Lotus Intelligent Driving integrates hardware, software, and cloud technologies to create a comprehensive suite of intelligent driving solutions, enabling vehicles to perceive their environments accurately and respond dynamically, thereby enhancing the driving experience even under challenging conditions.
